daily_brief: Sunday saw major developments across multiple fronts. In Washington, a gunman was killed by Secret Service agents after opening fire near the White House; President Trump was unharmed but the incident marks the third shooting near him in a month [BBC, France 24, Globe and Mail]. The US-Iran diplomatic track appears close to resolution, with Secretary of State Rubio indicating an announcement could come within hours, though Tehran disputes reports it has agreed to surrender enriched uranium stockpiles [France 24, Le Monde]. In Ukraine, Russia launched its largest strike on Kyiv in months, killing at least four and injuring over 80, with President Zelensky confirming the use of an Oreshnik hypersonic missile; the attack followed Putin's vow of retaliation for a strike on a dormitory in occupied Luhansk [BBC, France 24]. China's deadliest mining disaster since 2009 claimed at least 82 lives in Shanxi province after a gas explosion [BBC]. Meanwhile, the DRC's Ebola outbreak is accelerating rapidly, with suspected cases nearly tripling in a week to 750, prompting the WHO to raise its risk assessment and the Trump administration to pause deportations to the country [Guardian, Al Jazeera, WHO].

top_stories.headline: Gunman Killed by Secret Service After Opening Fire Near White House
bullets: A 21-year-old man identified as Nasire Best opened fire at a White House checkpoint at approximately 6pm local time on Saturday before being shot and killed by Secret Service agents
One bystander was wounded; no Secret Service officers were injured; President Trump was inside the White House but was not affected
The suspect was known to the Secret Service and had documented mental health issues; Trump stated the gunman had a 'violent history'
This marks the third shooting incident near President Trump in the past month, following incidents at the White House Correspondents' Dinner and near the Washington Monument

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized the suspect's mental health history and prior incidents

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on the timing amid Iran negotiations

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Highlighted this as third shooting near Trump in a month

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Content unavailable due to technical issues

top_stories.headline: Russia Launches Massive Missile and Drone Attack on Kyiv, Confirms Use of Oreshnik Hypersonic Missile
bullets: Russia struck Kyiv with approximately 600 drones and 90 missiles overnight Sunday, killing at least four people and injuring more than 80
President Zelensky confirmed Russia used the nuclear-capable Oreshnik intermediate-range hypersonic ballistic missile in the strikes
Mayor Klitschko reported damage across all districts of Kyiv including residential buildings, schools, and historic Independence Square
The attack followed Putin's vow to retaliate after accusing Ukraine of striking a student dormitory in Starobilsk, Luhansk, killing 21 people on Friday

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Provided detailed context on the Starobilsk strike dispute and Russian accusations

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Led with casualty figures and Oreshnik confirmation

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Content unavailable due to technical issues

top_stories.headline: At Least 82 Dead in China's Worst Coal Mine Disaster Since 2009
bullets: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu Coal Mine in Shanxi province killed at least 82 workers, with 2 still missing
247 workers were on duty when the blast occurred at 7:29pm local time Friday; over 100 were rescued
128 people remain hospitalized, most suffering from inhaling poisonous gas, with two in critical condition
President Xi Jinping ordered maximum rescue efforts; the state council announced a 'rigorous' investigation with those responsible to be 'severely punished'

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Noted confusion over initial death toll figures and deployed mine inspection robots

top_stories.headline: US-Iran Deal Reported Near Completion as Rubio Signals Imminent Announcement
bullets: Secretary of State Rubio stated an announcement on Iran was possible 'in the next few hours' while speaking in New Delhi
A potential deal reportedly includes restoration of Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic to pre-war levels within 30 days
US media reported the deal would include Iran handing over highly enriched uranium stockpiles, but an Iranian source denied any such agreement
Trump cited the Iran negotiations as reason for remaining in Washington and missing his son's wedding in the Bahamas

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Detailed both US and Iranian positions, noted Iranian denial of uranium handover

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Content unavailable but headline referenced deal as 'largely negotiated'

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Focused on Trump missing his son's wedding due to negotiations

top_stories.headline: Ebola Outbreak Accelerates in DRC as WHO Raises Risk Assessment
bullets: Suspected Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo have nearly tripled in one week to approximately 750, with 177 suspected deaths
The WHO raised its risk assessment for DRC to 'very high' and warned the outbreak is spreading rapidly across multiple areas
Health facilities have come under attack from residents angry about Ebola burial protocols, with a treatment center in Rwampara stormed and tents set on fire in Mongbwalu
The Trump administration announced a temporary pause on deportations to DRC due to the outbreak, though one deportee remains stranded in Kinshasa

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Emphasized healthcare system strain and deportation policy implications

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Focused on attacks against health workers and community mistrust
